question: [File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / _updateG] Can an unprivileged StabilityPool depositor under the precondition that latestRewardPerSec * elapsed_seconds > sableToken.balanceOf(communityIssuance) trigger the sequence provideToSP() -> _triggerSABLEIssuance() -> issueSABLE() returns issuance > actual_balance -> _updateG() inflates epochToScaleToG[currentEpoch][currentScale] -> depositor's computed SABLE gain exceeds actual SABLE held, violating the invariant that totalSABLEIssued <= sableToken.balanceOf(communityIssuance) at all times, causing scoped impact: SABLE reward over-claim where early claimants drain the contract and late claimants receive 0? Local proof idea: deploy fuzz test with latestRewardPerSec set to exhaust balance in T seconds, warp T+1, have two depositors claim sequentially, assert second depositor's sendSABLE() reverts and first depositor received more than their proportional share.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: sendSABLE] Can an unprivileged StabilityPool depositor under the precondition that the G accumulator has been inflated beyond the actual SABLE balance of CommunityIssuance trigger the sequence withdrawFromSP() -> _payOutSABLEGains() -> communityIssuance.sendSABLE(depositor, computedGain) where computedGain > sableToken.balanceOf(communityIssuance), violating the invariant that sendSABLE must never transfer more SABLE than the contract holds, causing scoped impact: sableToken.transfer() reverts and all subsequent SP withdrawals are permanently bricked? Local proof idea: unit test that sets latestRewardPerSec to exhaust balance, warps time, calls withdrawFromSP, asserts revert and measures which depositors are blocked.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / totalSABLEIssued] Can an unprivileged observer under the precondition that no cap exists on totalSABLEIssued relative to sableToken.balanceOf(communityIssuance) trigger the sequence: multiple provideToSP() calls over time -> each call increments totalSABLEIssued by latestRewardPerSec * dt -> totalSABLEIssued grows past actual balance, violating the invariant totalSABLEIssued <= sableToken.balanceOf(communityIssuance) + cumulative_sendSABLE_amounts, causing scoped impact: accounting divergence that enables first-mover SABLE extraction at the expense of later claimants? Local proof idea: invariant fuzz test asserting totalSABLEIssued <= communityIssuance.balanceSABLE() + sum_of_all_sendSABLE_calls after every state transition.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / _updateG / _computeSABLEPerUnitStaked] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition of a single tiny deposit (e.g. 1 wei USDS) in the StabilityPool trigger the sequence: provideToSP(1) -> issueSABLE() returns large issuance -> _computeSABLEPerUnitStaked(large_issuance, 1) -> SABLEPerUnitStaked = large_issuance * 1e18 / 1 overflows SafeMath, violating the invariant that SABLEPerUnitStaked must be representable in uint256, causing scoped impact: permanent DoS on all StabilityPool operations that trigger issuance, blocking all SABLE reward claims? Local proof idea: fuzz test with totalUSDSDeposits = 1 and latestRewardPerSec = 1e18, warp 1e18 seconds, call provideToSP, assert SafeMath overflow revert.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / _updateG] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that totalUSDSDeposits == 0 when issueSABLE() is called trigger the sequence: all depositors withdraw -> totalUSDSDeposits = 0 -> time passes -> new depositor calls provideToSP() -> _triggerSABLEIssuance() -> issueSABLE() returns large issuance for the zero-deposit period -> _updateG() skips update because totalUSDS == 0 -> lastIssuanceTime advances -> SABLE for the zero-deposit window is permanently unclaimable, violating the invariant that all SABLE issued must be claimable by depositors, causing scoped impact: SABLE stranded in CommunityIssuance that no depositor can ever claim? Local proof idea: unit test that empties SP, warps 1 day, re-deposits, asserts G did not increase and SABLE balance of communityIssuance did not decrease.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / _computeSABLEPerUnitStaked / lastSABLEError] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition of controlling deposit timing and size trigger the sequence: deposit large amount -> issueSABLE() called -> _computeSABLEPerUnitStaked sets lastSABLEError to a large value -> withdraw -> re-deposit tiny amount -> next issueSABLE() call uses inflated lastSABLEError in SABLENumerator = issuance * 1e18 + lastSABLEError -> SABLEPerUnitStaked is inflated -> attacker's G snapshot captures inflated G, violating the invariant that lastSABLEError must never cause a depositor to receive more SABLE than their proportional share, causing scoped impact: SABLE over-extraction via error accumulator manipulation? Local proof idea: differential fuzz test comparing SABLE gains with and without lastSABLEError manipulation, assert attacker gain <= proportional entitlement.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: updateRewardPerSec / ownerTriggerIssuance / issueSABLE] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that updateRewardPerSec() is called by owner in the same block as a StabilityPool operation trigger the sequence: StabilityPool.provideToSP() -> issueSABLE() sets lastIssuanceTime = block.timestamp -> owner calls updateRewardPerSec() -> stabilityPool.ownerTriggerIssuance() -> issueSABLE() called again with timeSinceLastIssue = 0 -> issuance = 0 -> require(lastIssuanceTime == block.timestamp) passes -> latestRewardPerSec updated, violating the invariant that the rate change must always be preceded by a non-zero issuance flush, causing scoped impact: rate change without flushing accrued rewards at old rate, allowing attacker to claim rewards at new higher rate for time that should have been at old rate? Local proof idea: unit test that calls provideToSP then updateRewardPerSec in same block, assert G reflects old rate for elapsed period.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / _updateG / epochToScaleToG] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition of a scale change (currentScale increments) trigger the sequence: deposit before scale change -> liquidation causes scale change -> issueSABLE() called -> _updateG() writes to epochToScaleToG[currentEpoch][newScale] -> depositor's _getSABLEGainFromSnapshots() computes secondPortion = epochToScaleToG[epochSnapshot][scaleSnapshot+1] / SCALE_FACTOR -> secondPortion captures SABLE issued after scale change at 1/1e9 precision, violating the invariant that SABLE gains must be computed with full precision across scale boundaries, causing scoped impact: depositor loses SABLE gains due to 1e9 precision truncation at scale boundary? Local proof idea: unit test with controlled liquidation causing scale change, compare depositor's actual SABLE gain to theoretical entitlement, assert loss < 1e9 wei.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: sendSABLE / _payOutSABLEGains] Can an unprivileged front-end operator under the precondition of registering with kickbackRate = 0 (front-end takes 100% of SABLE) trigger the sequence: registerFrontEnd(0) -> depositor calls provideToSP(amount, frontEndAddress) -> time passes -> depositor calls withdrawFromSP() -> _payOutSABLEGains() -> getFrontEndSABLEGain(frontEnd) returns 100% of SABLE earned by tagged deposits -> communityIssuance.sendSABLE(frontEnd, fullAmount) -> depositor receives 0 SABLE, violating the invariant that depositors must receive at least kickbackRate fraction of their earned SABLE, causing scoped impact: front-end operator extracts 100% of depositor SABLE rewards? Local proof idea: unit test with kickbackRate=0, assert depositorSABLEGain == 0 and frontEndSABLEGain == total earned.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: sendSABLE / _payOutSABLEGains] Can an unprivileged front-end contract under the precondition that _payOutSABLEGains() sends SABLE to front-end before depositor trigger the sequence: front-end is a malicious contract -> depositor calls withdrawFromSP() -> _payOutSABLEGains() calls communityIssuance.sendSABLE(frontEnd, frontEndGain) -> sableToken.transfer(frontEnd, frontEndGain) -> SABLEToken._requireValidRecipient() does NOT block frontEnd address -> front-end contract's fallback executes -> front-end reenters withdrawFromSP() or provideToSP() before depositor's SABLE is sent, violating the invariant that state must be fully settled before external calls, causing scoped impact: reentrancy enabling double-claim of SABLE rewards? Local proof idea: deploy malicious front-end contract that reenters on SABLE receipt, assert depositor SABLE gain is double-counted.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / _updateG / getDepositorSABLEGain] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that their deposit spans an epoch change (pool-emptying liquidation) trigger the sequence: deposit in epoch N -> pool-emptying liquidation -> currentEpoch = N+1 -> issueSABLE() called in epoch N+1 -> _updateG() writes to epochToScaleToG[N+1][0] -> depositor's _getSABLEGainFromSnapshots() reads epochToScaleToG[N][oldScale] which is the final G of epoch N -> depositor claims SABLE earned during epoch N even though their deposit was fully liquidated, violating the invariant that a fully liquidated deposit earns no SABLE after liquidation, causing scoped impact: SABLE over-claim by depositors whose deposits were fully consumed in a pool-emptying liquidation? Local proof idea: unit test with pool-emptying liquidation, assert depositor SABLE gain equals only pre-liquidation portion.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that latestRewardPerSec.mul(timeSinceLastIssue) overflows uint256 trigger the sequence: long period of inactivity (e.g. years) -> provideToSP() -> _triggerSABLEIssuance() -> issueSABLE() -> latestRewardPerSec.mul(timeSinceLastIssue) reverts via SafeMath overflow, violating the invariant that issueSABLE() must always succeed when called by StabilityPool, causing scoped impact: permanent DoS on all StabilityPool deposit/withdrawal/liquidation operations that trigger issuance? Local proof idea: fuzz test with latestRewardPerSec = 1e18 and warp block.timestamp by 1e18 seconds, assert SafeMath overflow revert.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / totalSABLEIssued.add(issuance)] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that totalSABLEIssued is near type(uint256).max trigger the sequence: totalSABLEIssued accumulates over time -> next issueSABLE() call -> totalSABLEIssued.add(issuance) overflows SafeMath -> revert, violating the invariant that totalSABLEIssued.add() must never revert, causing scoped impact: permanent DoS on all StabilityPool operations once totalSABLEIssued approaches uint256 max? Local proof idea: unit test that sets totalSABLEIssued to type(uint256).max - 1 via storage manipulation, calls issueSABLE, asserts revert.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: sendSABLE] Can an unprivileged StabilityPool depositor under the precondition that _SABLEamount == 0 trigger the sequence: withdrawFromSP() -> _payOutSABLEGains() -> communityIssuance.sendSABLE(depositor, 0) -> sableToken.transfer(depositor, 0) succeeds (ERC20 allows zero transfers) -> no SABLE moved but gas wasted, violating the invariant that sendSABLE with zero amount should be a no-op, causing scoped impact: unnecessary external call that could interact with a malicious SABLE token implementation or waste gas in a way that enables griefing? Local proof idea: unit test asserting sendSABLE(addr, 0) does not revert and emits no Transfer event.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / _updateG / _computeSABLEPerUnitStaked] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition of being the sole depositor with a large deposit trigger the sequence: sole depositor with 1e27 USDS -> issueSABLE() returns small issuance -> _computeSABLEPerUnitStaked(small, 1e27) -> SABLEPerUnitStaked rounds to 0 due to integer division -> lastSABLEError accumulates the full issuance -> G is not updated -> depositor receives 0 SABLE for that period, violating the invariant that all issued SABLE must eventually be claimable, causing scoped impact: SABLE permanently stranded in CommunityIssuance due to rounding to zero in large-deposit scenarios? Local proof idea: unit test with totalUSDSDeposits = 1e27 and issuance = 1e9, assert SABLEPerUnitStaked == 0 and lastSABLEError == 1e9 * 1e18.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / _updateG / marginalSABLEGain] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that SABLEPerUnitStaked * P overflows uint256 trigger the sequence: large issuance -> _computeSABLEPerUnitStaked returns large SABLEPerUnitStaked -> _updateG() computes marginalSABLEGain = SABLEPerUnitStaked.mul(P) -> SafeMath overflow reverts, violating the invariant that _updateG must always succeed, causing scoped impact: permanent DoS on all StabilityPool operations that trigger SABLE issuance? Local proof idea: fuzz test with SABLEPerUnitStaked near 1e18 and P = 1e18, assert marginalSABLEGain computation does not overflow.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / lastIssuanceTime] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that block.timestamp < lastIssuanceTime (impossible in normal operation but possible via chain reorg or timestamp manipulation) trigger the sequence: issueSABLE() -> timeSinceLastIssue = block.timestamp.sub(lastIssuanceTime) -> SafeMath underflow reverts, violating the invariant that issueSABLE must always succeed when called by StabilityPool, causing scoped impact: DoS on all StabilityPool operations if lastIssuanceTime is ever set ahead of block.timestamp? Local proof idea: unit test that sets lastIssuanceTime = block.timestamp + 1 via storage slot manipulation, calls issueSABLE, asserts SafeMath underflow revert.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: updateRewardPerSec / stabilityPool.ownerTriggerIssuance] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that updateRewardPerSec() is called when latestRewardPerSec is very high and a long time has elapsed trigger the sequence: owner calls updateRewardPerSec(newRate) -> stabilityPool.ownerTriggerIssuance() -> _triggerSABLEIssuance() -> issueSABLE() returns huge issuance -> _updateG() inflates G massively -> require(lastIssuanceTime == block.timestamp) passes -> latestRewardPerSec = newRate -> all existing depositors' SABLE gains are computed against the inflated G, violating the invariant that G inflation must be bounded by actual SABLE balance, causing scoped impact: depositors claim SABLE gains that exceed the contract's actual balance? Local proof idea: unit test that sets high rate, warps time, calls updateRewardPerSec, asserts sum of all depositor SABLE gains <= communityIssuance.balanceSABLE().

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / _updateG / epochToScaleToG overflow] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition of repeated large issuance events trigger the sequence: multiple provideToSP() calls over time -> each call adds marginalSABLEGain to epochToScaleToG[currentEpoch][currentScale] -> epochToScaleToG accumulates past type(uint256).max -> SafeMath overflow reverts, violating the invariant that epochToScaleToG must never overflow, causing scoped impact: permanent DoS on all StabilityPool operations once G accumulator overflows? Local proof idea: fuzz test with large latestRewardPerSec and many time warps, assert epochToScaleToG never overflows.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / _getSABLEGainFromSnapshots] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that their G snapshot is from a previous scale trigger the sequence: deposit at scale N -> scale changes to N+1 -> issueSABLE() called -> G updated at scale N+1 -> depositor's _getSABLEGainFromSnapshots() computes secondPortion = epochToScaleToG[epoch][N+1] / SCALE_FACTOR -> secondPortion is truncated by 1e9 -> depositor receives less SABLE than entitled, violating the invariant that SABLE gains must be computed without precision loss across scale changes, causing scoped impact: systematic SABLE under-payment to depositors spanning scale boundaries? Local proof idea: unit test with controlled scale change, compute theoretical SABLE gain, assert actual gain >= theoretical - 1e9.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / _updateG / _getSABLEGainFromSnapshots] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition of depositing just before a large issuance event trigger the sequence: deposit tiny amount -> issueSABLE() returns large issuance -> _updateG() inflates G -> depositor's SABLE gain = initialDeposit * (G - G_snapshot) / P_snapshot -> with tiny deposit and large G delta, gain is proportionally correct but absolute value is small -> attacker with large deposit at same time extracts disproportionate SABLE, violating the invariant that SABLE distribution must be strictly proportional to deposit size, causing scoped impact: large depositors extract SABLE at the expense of small depositors via timing? Local proof idea: differential test comparing SABLE gain ratios for two depositors of different sizes, assert ratio equals deposit ratio.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: sendSABLE / sableToken.transfer] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that the SABLE token's _requireValidRecipient blocks certain addresses trigger the sequence: _payOutSABLEGains() -> communityIssuance.sendSABLE(depositor, amount) -> sableToken.transfer(depositor, amount) -> _requireValidRecipient(depositor) reverts if depositor is a blocked address (e.g. sableStakingAddress), violating the invariant that sendSABLE must always succeed for valid depositor addresses, causing scoped impact: SABLE gains permanently locked for depositors whose address matches a blocked recipient in SABLEToken? Local proof idea: unit test where depositor address == sableStakingAddress, call withdrawFromSP, assert revert in sendSABLE.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / _updateG / provideToSP repeated] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition of repeatedly calling provideToSP() in the same block trigger the sequence: provideToSP() -> issueSABLE() with timeSinceLastIssue = 0 -> issuance = 0 -> _updateG(0) returns early -> G not updated -> depositor's snapshot captures current G -> repeat -> no SABLE issued for any of these calls, violating the invariant that SABLE issuance must be continuous and not skippable, causing scoped impact: attacker can reset their G snapshot to current G without triggering issuance, effectively zeroing out their SABLE liability? Local proof idea: unit test calling provideToSP twice in same block, assert G unchanged and depositor's snapshot G equals current G.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / _updateG / getFrontEndSABLEGain] Can an unprivileged front-end operator under the precondition of having a non-zero frontEndStake but a stale snapshot trigger the sequence: front-end has deposits tagged -> time passes -> issueSABLE() called multiple times -> G increases -> front-end never updates snapshot -> front-end calls withdrawFromSP() on behalf of depositor -> _payOutSABLEGains() -> getFrontEndSABLEGain() computes gain on stale frontEndStake -> front-end receives SABLE for the full G delta since last snapshot, violating the invariant that front-end SABLE gain must equal (1-kickbackRate) * depositor_earned_SABLE, causing scoped impact: front-end over-claims SABLE if frontEndStake diverges from actual tagged deposits? Local proof idea: unit test where depositors withdraw reducing actual tagged deposits but frontEndStake is not updated, assert getFrontEndSABLEGain <= actual entitlement.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: issueSABLE / _updateG / _updateFrontEndStakeAndSnapshots] Can an unprivileged front-end operator under the precondition that frontEndStake is updated independently of depositor compounded deposits trigger the sequence: depositor A tags to frontEnd -> liquidation reduces A's compounded deposit -> A calls provideToSP(topup) -> _updateFrontEndStakeAndSnapshots(frontEnd, compoundedFrontEndStake + topup) -> compoundedFrontEndStake = getCompoundedFrontEndStake(frontEnd) which uses frontEndStakes[frontEnd] * P / P_snapshot -> if frontEndStakes[frontEnd] was not reduced by liquidation, compoundedFrontEndStake > actual tagged deposits -> newFrontEndStake is inflated, violating the invariant that frontEndStake must equal sum of tagged depositors' compounded deposits, causing scoped impact: front-end SABLE over-claim due to frontEndStake inflation after liquidations? Local proof idea: invariant test asserting frontEndStake == sum(getCompoundedUSDSDeposit(d) for d tagged to frontEnd) after every liquidation.
